result:
I'll read the log file and identify the most common error patterns.
The file looks truncated in the read — I'll scan the full log for distinct error patterns.
The log has **200 ERROR lines**, and they all share the same structure. Here are the top 3 patterns:

- **`timeout after 30000ms`** — 200 occurrences (100% of errors); every line is a 30s request timeout.
- **`path=/api/v2/orders/{id}`** — 200 occurrences; all failures are on the orders API (`/api/v2/orders/1` through `/api/v2/orders/200`).
- **`payload={"id":N,"status":"failed"}`** — 200 occurrences; each timeout is tied to an order with `"status":"failed"`.

In short, this log is one repeated error pattern across 200 services/orders, not three distinct error types.
